{"key_event":"Managed democratic transition under authoritarian enclaves","reasoning":"Civil society recovered markedly through the 1988 No campaign, elections, parties, NGOs, local democracy, and human-rights activism, but labor weakness, demobilization, and institutional vetoes kept it below its pre-1973 strength. State capacity remained very strong through legal continuity, bureaucracy, growth, revenues, and military coercive power, though elected civilian authority was constrained by Pinochet’s military autonomy and constitutional enclaves.","society_change":1.7,"society_power":6.7,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":8.6}
